data.title: Colliers appoints Ludovic Delaisse as CEO of Colliers France to enhance company's position in EMEA.
data.description: Today, on January 23, 2026, Colliers has announced Ludovic Delaisse as the new Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of Colliers France. This strategic appointment is part of a planned leadership transition aimed at enhancing Colliers' presence in France and throughout EMEA (Europe, the Middle East, and Africa). Delaisse's new role is effective immediately, signifying a pivotal move for Colliers in the region.
Bekaert has announced an update on its share buyback program and liquidity agreement.

data.title: Okeanis Eco Tankers Corp issued 3,611,111 new shares at $36 each, raising about $130 million in proceeds.
data.description: In Athens, Greece on January 23, 2026, Okeanis Eco Tankers Corp. announced the successful offering of 3,611,111 new common shares at a price of USD 36.00 per share. The offering raised approximately USD 130 million in gross proceeds. The company is listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange under the ticker code "OET" and on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker code "ECO".

data.title: Performance Brokerage Services facilitates sale of David Taylor Ellisville CDJR in Missouri to Bommarito Automotive Group.
data.description: A prominent in dealership buy-sell transactions, Performance Brokerage Services revealed the recent sale of David Taylor Ellisville Chrysler Dodge Jeep Ram in Ellisville, Missouri. The dealership changed hands from David Taylor to Bommarito Automotive Group located in Irvine, California. This transaction, announced on January 23, 2026, showcases the ongoing trend in the automotive industry.

data.title: GKGD introduces new LED display portfolio at ISE 2026 in Barcelona, China, featuring diverse applications. #LEDdisplay #ISE2026
data.description: On Jan. 23, 2026, in Barcelona, Spain and Changzhi, China, GKGD, a top Chinese LED display maker, announced its participation in ISE 2026, the biggest professional expo in Europe for audiovisual and systems integration. The company plans to exhibit a wide variety of LED products at the event.

data.title: Wall Street analysts predict Booz Allen Hamilton (BAH) will report Q3 earnings of $1.27/share, down from $1.55/share last year; revenue estimate is $2.73 billion (down from $2.92 billion in previous year). Company recently partnered with Andreessen Horowitz for advancing technology. BAH stock closed at $95.76, rising by 0.2%.
data.description: Booz Allen Hamilton Holding Corporation is set to announce its third quarter earnings before the market opens on Friday, January 23. Analysts are predicting that the company, headquartered in McLean, Virginia, will report earnings of $1.27 per share for the fourth quarter, a decrease from $1.55 per share in the same period last year. The consensus estimate for the company's quarterly revenue stands at $2.73 billion, compared to $2.92 billion reported last year. This information comes from Benzinga Pro. Booz Allen Hamilton recently revealed a partnership with Andreessen Horowitz aimed at advancing and expanding technology solutions for Governments. The company's stock closed at $95.76 on Thursday, up 0.2%. For more details and the full article, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Blind sailor Mitsuhiro Iwamoto spoke at WEF about solo trans-Pacific crossing project in 2027 sailing a 28-ft yacht.
data.description: In Fujisawa, Japan, Mitsuhiro Iwamoto, a visually impaired Japanese sailor residing in San Diego, made a presentation at the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting of 2026 on January 21. He is embarking on the pioneering mission of accomplishing the first-ever solo, non-stop trans-Pacific voyage by a blind individual as a part of the "Blind Sailor Single-Handed Pacific Crossing Project 2027." The upcoming challenge in the spring of 2027 involves a solo, non-stop trans-Pacific journey aboard a 28-foot yacht. For more information, visit the World Economic Forum's website at https://www.weforum.org/.

data.title: Dow gains 300+ points on easing geopolitical concerns. Fear & Greed Index remains neutral. Stocks rise after Trump tariff suspension announcements. Meta Platforms Inc (NASDAQ: META) up 5%, Procter & Gamble (NYSE: PG) up 3%. General Aerospace (NYSE: GE) also mentioned.
data.description: The CNN Money Fear and Greed index signaled slight improvement in market sentiment, remaining in the "Neutral" zone on Thursday. U.S. stocks ended higher, with the Dow Jones index rising over 300 points due to reduced geopolitical tensions. The positive shift came after Trump's announcement to halt proposed tariffs on European nations, citing a framework deal from NATO discussions. Meta Platforms Inc. (NASDAQ: META) saw a 5% increase following Jefferies' mention of an appealing valuation discount. Procter & Gamble (NYSE: PG) surged by approximately 3% after posting better-than-expected earnings. General Aerospace (NYSE: GE)... Full article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: One Heung Kong in Guangzhou earns three international certifications for sustainability and architecture.
data.description: In Guangzhou, China, on January 21, 2026, One Heung Kong, a top-tier luxury residence in the Greater Bay Area, has reached a significant accomplishment in sustainable architecture. The property has successfully obtained certification from the World Green Design Organization (WGDO) with the prestigious LEED Platinum rating. This achievement showcases the commitment of One Heung Kong towards environmentally-friendly practices and designing buildings that adhere to green standards.
